constructs: moral valence side-effect attribution intentionality pragmatic-effect
claim: Moral considerations reshape how agents' actions are semantically classified, independent of the causal structure or pragmatic communication context.

Discrimination by street-level bureaucrats depends on both individual bias and institutional/community context; some minority groups face bias in tone but not access, and contextual diversity dampens but does not eliminate disparities.
Naturally occurring visual perceptual biases—contraction bias and Weber's law—systematically reduce ability to detect obesity and weight gain, especially in heavier individuals.

Implicit theories of intelligence shape achievement trajectories by organizing a coherent framework of goals, effort attribution, and coping strategies that matter most when external support declines.
Teaching students that intelligence is malleable can interrupt downward achievement trajectories by increasing classroom effort and learning motivation, even within a single semester.

Early visual system extracts three-dimensional orientation of simple convex objects preattentively and in parallel, suggesting cognitive processing of volumetric structure occurs before focused attention.
